name: nix-embedded-languages
description: Syntax highlighting for embedded languages in Nix multi-line strings.

nix-embedded-languages.include.description: Add custom languages. Keys use identifier characters plus the |, *, +, and ? regex operators only; escape those operators if you need literal matching. Values specify the language TextMate scope name, or an object with properties `name` and `scopeName` (required).

nix-embedded-languages.functionBindings.description: Map a Nix function name pattern to a language identifier from the built-in languages or `include` config. Patterns allow identifier characters plus the |, *, +, and ? regex operators only; escape those operators if you need literal matching. When the function is called with a `''` multiline string argument on the same line, the string content is automatically highlighted as that language — no `# lang` marker needed. Built-in bindings (writeShellScript, writeShellScriptBin, writeScript, writeScriptBin, writeBash, writeBashBin, writeDash -> shell) are merged with this config; user keys override.

nix-embedded-languages.variableMarkers.p.description: Map variable name prefixes to language identifiers from the built-in languages or `include` config. For example, { "script": "shell" } highlights variables like `scriptFoo = ''` as shell. Markers allow identifier characters plus the |, *, +, and ? regex operators only; escape those operators if you need literal matching.

nix-embedded-languages.variableMarkers.s.description: Map variable name suffixes to language identifiers from the built-in languages or `include` config. For example, { "Script": "shell" } highlights variables like `fooScript = ''` as shell. Markers allow identifier characters plus the |, *, +, and ? regex operators only; escape those operators if you need literal matching.

nix-embedded-languages.enableFunctionBin.description: Enable built-in function-call bindings (writeShellScript, writeBash, writeDash, etc. -> shell). Set to false to disable only the built-in bindings; user-defined `functionBindings` still apply.
